description: Flaveur is the defining fine-dining address in Nice, combining the Tourteaux brothers’ globally inflected creativity with the city’s strongest current Michelin standing. For luxury travelers, it offers the clearest blend of culinary prestige, polish, and destination-worthy seriousness in central Nice.
highlights: Nice’s flagship two-star table with creative Mediterranean cooking and top-tier prestige.
sources: Michelin Guide; Best of Nice; official website; Elite Traveler

description: Le Chantecler remains Nice’s grandest formal dining experience, set inside the legendary Le Negresco on the Promenade des Anglais. Its mix of palace-hotel glamour, heritage interiors, and Virginie Basselot’s refined cuisine makes it essential for affluent diners seeking Riviera classicism at the highest level.
highlights: Michelin-starred grande dame inside Le Negresco with historic decor and Riviera palace glamour.
sources: Michelin Guide; Le Negresco; official website; Nice Cote d'Azur Tourist Office

description: JAN is one of the Riviera’s most distinctive small-format luxury restaurants, pairing Jan Hendrik van der Westhuizen’s South African point of view with polished French technique and a highly personal sense of hospitality. Its intimate scale, imaginative experience, and affluent international following make it one of Nice’s most compelling reservations.
highlights: Michelin-starred intimate destination with South African-French identity and highly personal luxury appeal.
sources: Michelin Guide; official website; Best of Nice

description: Les Agitateurs brings a stylish, highly modern energy to Nice fine dining, balancing Michelin-starred culinary precision with a charming small-scale setting near the port. It is especially relevant for design-conscious diners who want serious food without the heaviness of old-school formal luxury.
highlights: Michelin-starred modern favorite near the port with strong atmosphere and contemporary appeal.
sources: Michelin Guide; official website; Best of Nice

description: Pure & V is one of Nice’s most commercially relevant Michelin-starred restaurants because it combines exacting cooking with a wine program that carries real destination value. The mood is polished yet contemporary, making it a natural choice for affluent diners who prioritize food and wine in equal measure.
highlights: Michelin-starred dining room with standout wine program and sleek contemporary feel.
sources: Michelin Guide; Best of Nice; official website

description: ONICE is one of the most current luxury tables in Nice, translating Mirazur-trained talent into a compact, sharply contemporary dining experience in the port district. It feels confident, design-aware, and highly relevant to affluent travelers looking for the city’s newer generation of Michelin dining.
highlights: Michelin-starred port-district restaurant with current design language and strong chef pedigree.
sources: Michelin Guide; Best of Nice; official website

description: L'Aromate remains one of Nice’s smartest one-star reservations, offering sleek interiors, open-kitchen energy, and elegant modern cooking close to Place Massena. It appeals strongly to luxury diners who want culinary credibility in a more urban and contemporary setting than the city’s heritage hotel institutions.
highlights: Michelin-starred city-center address with sleek interiors and polished modern cuisine.
sources: Michelin Guide; official website; Best of Nice

description: Racines Bruno Cirino is one of the most distinctive Michelin-starred restaurants in France, pairing Bruno Cirino’s celebrated culinary intelligence with a deeply local, vegetable-led Riviera approach. Its small scale and anti-flash character make it less overtly glamorous, but for sophisticated diners it is one of Nice’s most meaningful high-end experiences.
highlights: Michelin-starred vegetable-led destination with singular culinary identity and serious gourmet credibility.
sources: Michelin Guide; Best of Nice; official website

description: Epicentre is the breakout luxury dining story in Nice, earning a Michelin star almost immediately and positioning itself as the city’s most talked-about new gastronomic table. The open-kitchen format, spice-driven identity, and chef’s-table energy make it particularly compelling for affluent diners seeking something fresh and high-conviction.
highlights: New Michelin-starred sensation with immersive open-kitchen dining and a distinctive spice-led concept.
sources: Michelin Guide; official website; Best of Nice; Nice Cote d'Azur Tourist Office

description: Le Bistrot de JAN translates Jan Hendrik’s world into a more approachable but still highly polished format, with strong design cues and a warm, fashionable atmosphere. It is one of the best examples in Nice of a luxury bistro that genuinely competes with starred dining for affluent locals and travelers.
highlights: Stylish luxury bistro from the JAN team with South African-French flair and strong social appeal.
sources: Michelin Guide; official website; Best of Nice

description: Keisuke Matsushima remains one of Nice’s most sophisticated chef-driven restaurants even after moving beyond Michelin-star status. The cuisine’s Japanese precision, Riviera sourcing, and elegant dining room make it a highly credible luxury alternative for diners who value technique, loyalty, and a calmer form of refinement.
highlights: Chef-driven refined address blending Japanese precision with Riviera ingredients in an elegant setting.
sources: official website; Best of Nice; Nice Cote d'Azur Tourist Office

description: La Reserve de Nice is one of the city’s most visually compelling luxury restaurants, with panoramic views over the Baie des Anges and a distinctly Riviera sense of occasion. It is exactly the sort of place affluent diners consider instead of Michelin dining when atmosphere, sea views, and polished service matter just as much as technical prestige.
highlights: Sea-view gastronomic address with Art Deco character and major sunset appeal over the Bay of Angels.
sources: Michelin Guide; official website; Destination Cote d'Azur

description: Le Plongeoir is one of the most iconic restaurant settings on the Riviera, suspended above the sea on the diving platform at the edge of the port. While it is not a Michelin-style gastronomic room, its singular setting, romantic atmosphere, and polished Mediterranean positioning make it indispensable to the real luxury dining market in Nice.
highlights: Iconic over-the-water restaurant with dramatic sea setting and strong destination appeal.
sources: official website; Falstaff; Time Out

description: SEEN by Olivier is one of Nice’s clearest luxury power-dining addresses, pairing a rooftop setting, cocktails, music, and Riviera views with a globally legible high-spend concept. For affluent international guests, it is an easy and highly relevant choice when the brief is social energy, glamour, and polished hospitality.
highlights: Rooftop luxury restaurant with cocktails, sea views, and strong international social appeal.
sources: official website; Anantara Plaza Nice Hotel

description: La Petite Maison is a Riviera institution whose celebrity following, terrace energy, and polished Mediterranean menu give it lasting relevance well beyond the Michelin conversation. It remains one of the most commercially important bookings in Nice for affluent diners who want social recognition, familiarity, and a classic Cours Saleya atmosphere.
highlights: Legendary Riviera institution with celebrity cachet, terrace buzz, and enduring luxury-market relevance.
sources: Best of Nice; official website

description: NESPO is one of Nice’s strongest affluent-crowd favorites, bringing together polished service, lounge-like glamour, and a clientele that skews distinctly high-spend and image-conscious. It is especially relevant because it competes for the same luxury customer as the starred restaurants, but on mood, scene, and hospitality-led appeal.
highlights: Fashionable high-end favorite with lounge glamour, polished service, and strong affluent local following.
sources: official website; Best of Nice; Nice Presse

description: La Rotonde offers one of Nice’s most distinctive grand-brasserie experiences, framed by the whimsical carousel-inspired design and palace-hotel polish of Le Negresco. It is a strong luxury choice for diners who want heritage, a terrace on the Promenade, and a less formal but still highly elevated Riviera meal.
highlights: Iconic upscale brasserie at Le Negresco with terrace seating and strong palace-hotel appeal.
sources: Michelin Guide; Le Negresco; official website

description: Le 3e combines one of the city’s most dependable panoramic terrace views with the reassuring standards of the Hyatt Regency Palais de la Mediterranee. It is a particularly relevant luxury option for travelers and business diners who want sea views, polished service, and a refined hotel setting without full gastronomic formality.
highlights: Hotel terrace restaurant with panoramic bay views and polished Riviera-hotel dining appeal.
sources: Hyatt Regency Nice Palais de la Mediterranee; official website
hotel_name_hint: Hyatt Regency Nice Palais de la Mediterranee

description: Le Bistrot Gourmand sits just steps from the old town yet feels notably more polished than a typical city bistro, with white-tablecloth service, a serious cellar, and a loyal gourmet following. It is a highly credible luxury-bistro alternative for diners who want refinement, comfort, and consistency over spectacle.
highlights: Refined bistronomic address near Old Nice with white-tablecloth service and strong gourmet credibility.
sources: official website; Best of Nice; Nice Cote d'Azur Tourist Office

description: Le Restaurant du Couvent benefits from one of the most design-significant hotel openings on the Riviera, set inside the carefully restored Hotel du Couvent in Old Nice. The former convent architecture, gardens, and quietly luxurious hospitality make it highly relevant to affluent travelers drawn to beauty, atmosphere, and place-making as much as cuisine.
highlights: Design-led luxury hotel restaurant in a restored convent with strong architectural and lifestyle appeal.
sources: Hotel du Couvent; official website; Le Monde

description: Maison Bellet offers one of the most attractive countryside-style escapes within Nice itself, set on the hills with sea views, terrace dining, and a calmer luxury rhythm than the waterfront circuit. For affluent diners seeking a scenic lunch or a more residential-feeling dinner, it is one of the city’s strongest non-Michelin alternatives.
highlights: Hillside restaurant with sea-view terrace, polished Mediterranean cooking, and destination-lunch appeal.
sources: official website; Tripadvisor

description: Peixes Opera is one of Nice’s smartest upscale seafood options, channeling the city’s Mediterranean identity into a highly social, design-aware setting near the opera house. It is less formal than the starred restaurants, but for affluent diners wanting stylish seafood with real local credibility, it is an extremely relevant choice.
highlights: Stylish seafood address near the opera with vibrant Mediterranean atmosphere and insider appeal.
sources: Michelin Guide; official website

description: Bocca Nissa is one of Nice’s most commercially relevant luxury-med restaurants, pairing a lush rooftop, festive energy, and a prime Cours Saleya-adjacent address. It earns its place because affluent diners regularly choose it over more serious culinary rooms when they want atmosphere, design, and a Riviera night out with style.
highlights: Rooftop Mediterranean favorite with strong party energy and a prime Old Nice luxury-social location.
sources: official website; Nice Cote d'Azur Tourist Office

description: Babel Babel sits directly on the seafront and combines a design-conscious room, broad Mediterranean-Levantine menu, and strong day-to-night social energy. It is a fashionable luxury alternative rather than a gastronomic temple, but it fits the affluent Riviera customer set extremely well because of its setting, mood, and recognizability.
highlights: Seafront design-led restaurant with fashionable Mediterranean-Levantine style and strong social momentum.
sources: official website; Gault&Millau

description: Le Sejour Cafe is more discreet than Nice’s headline luxury addresses, but its contemporary room, carefully executed cooking, and strong repeat following make it a dependable insider recommendation. For affluent diners who prefer intimacy and culinary steadiness over spectacle, it is a meaningful part of the city’s high-end consideration set.
highlights: Contemporary intimate favorite with polished modern cooking and strong insider reputation.
sources: Michelin Guide; official website; Destination Cote d'Azur France
